Title: Database Services | Database Platform Reliability & Governance
Description:
This Epic encompasses top-down oversight of biBerk's database platforms (Microsoft SQL Server, Azure SQL and Cosmos DB), ensuring high availability, operational resilience, and effective governance at the infrastructure level. In a regulated insurance environment, this strategic approach focuses on the foundational stability of the platforms themselves—enabling secure and reliable data hosting, minimizing downtime and rapidly recovering from incidents—while remaining distinct from the management of actual data content, schemas, or application-specific logic (which resides in development purviews).


Key outcomes:

  • Proactive identification and mitigation of reliability risks
  • Standardized processes for maintenance, incident response, and change management
  • Clear ownership of database-specific operational concerns separate from application development

This Epic includes features related to monitoring, backups, high availability configurations, incident management, and routine maintenance activities.

Title: DBA | Proactive Monitoring & Alert Management
Description:
This Feature establishes top-down, platform-level monitoring and alerting for Microsoft SQL Server, Azure SQL Database and Cosmos DB environments. Focusing on infrastructure metrics, health signals, and automated reports and notifications, we ensure early detection of issues at the platform tier and database tier. This foundational oversight enables rapid response and minimizes disruptions across all workloads.


Key outcomes:  Configured baselines, tuned alerts, and regular review processes.

Title: DBA | Backup, Recovery & High Availability
Description:
From a platform perspective, this Feature defines, implements and maintains backup strategies, disaster recovery testing, and high-availability.  Emphasis is on the resilience of the database platforms and databases themselves. This approach promotes logical business continuity safeguards in a regulated environment.


Key outcomes: Documented RPO/RTO alignment, regular testing, and automated failover readiness.

Title: DBA | Incident Response & Root Cause Analysis
Description:
This Feature encompasses management processes for database-related events, including on-call procedures and post-incident reviews. The focus remains on platform behavior rather than application logic or data anomalies, enabling clear ownership and faster, more efficient resolution while supporting development teams.


Key outcomes: Standardized processes, efficient troubleshooting, and blameless RCA templates.

Title: DBA | Database Maintenance
Description:
This Feature manages scheduled maintenance windows, execution plan auditing, statistics updates, and regularly occurring optimization. Activities target platform health and performance baselines, separate from application-driven schema changes or data maintenance tasks.


Key outcomes: Automated routines and minimal-impact scheduling.

Title: Database Services | Database Performance & Cost Optimization
Description:
Again, aligning with a top-down perspective, this Epic drives the strategic optimization of biBerk's database platforms for peak performance, scalability, and cost-efficiency, supporting evolving workloads like Apollo and analytics. This approach emphasizes platform-level tuning and resource management—such as indexing strategies and Azure configurations—distinct from optimizing the underlying data content or application queries (which are effectively handled at the development level). 


By focusing on the "engine" rather than the "cargo," we promote faster, more economical operations across the organization, enabling development teams to deliver features faster without database bottlenecks.


Key outcomes:

  • Measurable query response times, throughput and latency at the infrastructure tier.
  • Reduced Azure database costs through right-sizing and efficient configurations
  • Proactive guidance on data modeling and indexing best practices

This Epic includes features around query tuning, indexing strategies, resource scaling, and ongoing performance reviews.

Title: DBA | Indexing Strategies & Query Performance Tuning
Description:
This Feature provides platform-level guidance and oversight on indexing, query execution plans, and general performance tuning for Microsoft SQL Server, Azure SQL and Cosmos DB.  While we review, provide deep insights and advise on trends, ownership remains focused on development-led efforts which rely upon application specific query logic and data access patterns.


Key outcomes: Standardized indexing guidelines, standardized metric analysis / review, etc.

Title: DBA | Resource Scaling & Cost Optimization
Description:
This Feature optimizes resource provisioning—CPU, RAM, disk-space, elastic pools, DTU / vCore sizing, Cosmos DB RU allocation, and auto-scaling—to balance performance and cost. Focus is on platform efficiency as a whole, enabling development workloads and application performance.


Key outcomes: Regular cost reviews and right-sizing recommendations.

Title: DBA | Schema & Data Model Reviews
Description:
This Feature conducts high-level reviews of proposed schema changes and data models from a platform governance standpoint, ensuring alignment with performance, scalability, and manageability standards. Reviews are advisory and platform-focused, distinct from detailed application data design ownership.


Key outcomes: Review checklist and feedback loop with development teams.

Title: Database Services | Database Security & Compliance Enablement
Description:
This Epic covers the strategic enablement, specialized security and compliance responsibilities for biBerk's database platforms which reside within a highly regulated insurance environment.  Prioritizing platform-level protections, we safeguard the environment as a whole, which contains the specific data that is stored and / or manipulated within it.  We ensure that data is protected, user access is appropriately controlled, auditing meets regulatory requirements, and vulnerabilities are promptly addressed—all while enabling secure development practices.  By securing the vault itself, not inventorying the contents, we help maintain regulatory alignment and proactively reduce enterprise risk.


Key outcomes:

  • Robust least-privilege access model integrated with Azure Entra ID / Active Directory
  • Comprehensive auditing and reporting capabilities
  • Timely remediation of security findings and alignment with industry standards

This Epic includes features related to identity / access management, auditing configurations, vulnerability assessments, and compliance reporting.

Title: DBA | Identity & User Access Management
Description:
This Feature implements and maintains platform-level identity integration (Entra ID / Azure AD), role-based access controls, , promotion of environment level discipline and least-privilege models for database environments. Scope is within the DBMS and Database security layers.


Key outcomes: Centralized access policies and regular audits.

Title: DBA | Auditing & Compliance Reporting
Description:
This Feature is related to promoting Product Owner / Solution SME security attestations and User Access Reviews (UAR) at the DBMS / DB layer.


Key outcomes:  Easily consumable metrics, configuration settings, and other valuable attributes regularly provided to facilitate regulatory compliance.

Title: DBA | Vulnerability Assessment & Remediation
Description:
From a top-down view, this Feature runs regular vulnerability scans, tracks findings, and applies platform-level remediations. Focus remains on database engine and configuration vulnerabilities, not application code or data exposure risks.


Key outcomes: Scheduled scans and remediation tracking.
